creator: merlyn500@gmail.com
text:
On Monday 12 January 2009 16:16:04 Kris Moore wrote:
> Milan wrote:
> > Hello,
> > on FreeBSD 7.1-PRERELEASE
> > uname -a:
> > FreeBSD vallhala.invalidnivozik.cz 7.1-PRERELEASE FreeBSD
> > 7.1-PRERELEASE #0: Sat Jan  3 22:19:21 CET 2009
> > root@vallhala.invalidnivozik.cz:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/LIGHT  i386
> >
> > dmesg:
>> [ ... dmesg snipped ... ]
> > ----------------------------------
> >
> >
> > It works OK. Does that problem appears only when starting game?
> > It seems, it hangs only because /home/kris/.btanks/bt.xml doesn't
> > exist. ([10:19:35.061][src/config.cpp:39] [error] load:
> > [mrt/file.cpp:36]: fopen("/home/kris/.btanks/bt.xml", "rt"): No
> > such file or directory)
> >
> >
> > I attach my bt.xml file, try to use this one.
>
> Ok, I did some further testing on this. Using your bt.xml file, did
> get the game to startup now. However, the first few maps I tried to
> play caused the game to crash again, with these errors:
>
> [10:01:18.742][src/main.cpp:73]  [error] main:
> [src/game_monitor.cpp:677]: this map requires lua scripting
> support. [10:01:18.742][src/main.cpp:73]  [debug] calling
> Game->deinit() [10:01:18.742][src/game.cpp:729]         [debug]
> cleaning up main game object...
>
Yes, this did me too, enabling lua is solve this problem (as you have 
written).

>
>
> I looked into the Makefile, and saw that the option for LUA was set
> to off. I enabled it and rebuilt the game. Now I can play games
> just fine.
>
> Since all the maps I tried require LUA, it would probably be best
> to set it to "on" by default.
Yes, lua should be enabled, you ought to contact maintainer on 
danfe@FreeBSD.org (I send this message to him too).

> Also, how can we fix or get around 
> the missing bt.xml files? 
I don't know. My bt.xml was created first time I had started game.

> Thats kind a showstopper if the user cant 
> run the game because its missing. 

> Does your game run if you remove 
> ~/.btanks/bt.xml?
Yes, and creates missing bt.xml, thats strange that yours doesn't. Do 
you have latest version? (PORTVERSION = 0.7.5800).

Regards,
Milan
